blocks.content: Kanis, how long are we going to stay here?

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: In the narrow chamber of the pyramid, Kanis and Arin sat side by side, leaning against the wall.
A considerable amount of time had passed, but neither had the will to move.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: It can't be,
This insane space is trying to drive us mad. Think about it—twins? Do you and I look anything alike?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: They did. Not identical, but as fraternal twins, it wasn't impossible.
Arin lowered her head.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: What's going to happen to us?

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: There's no point in thinking about it! Nothing changes. Arin, think about the years we've spent together!
Someone more precious than life itself.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: I want you. I wanted to spend my entire life with you. And now you"re telling me we share the same blood?
Overcome by anger, Kanis grabbed Arin's shoulders and kissed her roughly.
It was bold, but if it were Arin, she might have accepted it willingly.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: No!
But she pushed him away, and Kanis fell back onto the ground, looking stunned.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Why… why did you do that?
Reading Kanis's emotions through her empathy, she began to cry.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I love you too, but… just in case…

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: No! That's impossible! We agreed to live believing it wasn't true!
Though they'd never spoken the words aloud.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: We did, but now we know. At least, until we're completely sure…

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: You…
Kanis was about to shout in frustration when—

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: There you are.
From the darkness emerged a pair of glowing red eyes.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Ares,

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Is everyone safe? We need to find Shirone.
Muttering as he approached, Ares paused, surprised by the looks on their faces.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: What happened? Why do you both look so pale?
Neither of them answered.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: I see. It got to you too.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Ares, did you… see the past as well?

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Yes. I keep resetting it with my ability, but it's a horrible task. The timeline in this place is warped. The fact that we're all separated is proof of that.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Why is this happening to us?

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: There's no reason,
He examined the glowing runes running along the wall.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: This is just that kind of place. It seems all causality is dismantled here, regardless of time. It's almost like…

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: God's perspective?

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Exactly. From outside the universe, even time would look like just one thread. Maybe there really is a god living here.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: Let's go,
I'm going to meet them myself and find out why they're doing this to us. I'll fix it. If there really is a god…

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: He clenched his fists.

blocks.speaker: Kanis
blocks.content: I'll kill them if I have to.
Each member of the World Health Organization delegation had been assigned a private room.
Seriel, placing her large bag on the desk, was frustrated that her mood had been ruined so early in the morning.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Annoying,
The face of Fermi flashed in her mind.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: If Amy found out, she'd probably try to kill him.
Her cheeks burned at the thought, but another crossed her mind.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Would she, though?
Everyone has secrets.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: If one were to dismantle the causes and effects of a person's life, it would reveal countless impulses, shame, and fleeting joys beyond anyone's imagination.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Back then, we had no idea the world would turn out like this.
Sitting on the edge of her desk, she lit a cigarette.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Why did I start smoking?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Perhaps that, too, was just another… Cause.
It was during Seriel's final year at school.
The day after the 19th week of training, they were given a rare day off.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Strengthening my Spirit Zone. What about you?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: I'm thinking about grabbing some coffee.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Alright, then… let's go to the café.
Unlike the tireless Amy, Seriel was an ordinary person who needed rest.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: I'm thinking about grabbing some coffee.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Got it. Go to the bookstore.
Not wanting to interrupt Amy's training, Seriel found an excuse to slip away.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Haha, no way. It's a dangerous book. Someone without immunity like you would be in trouble. See you tomorrow!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hey! Seriel!
Amy called out urgently, but Seriel was already rounding the corner of the hallway.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Could she have stopped her?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: For a moment, the thought crossed her mind, but the tasks ahead left no room for distraction.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: …Back to training.
Meanwhile, Seriel, relieved not to have become a nuisance, sighed as she stepped outside.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: I'll just have some fun on my own.
Feeling free, she left the school and arrived at a run-down bookstore.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Hello.
Far from the noble district and occasionally receiving banned books, this was her little hideout.

blocks.speaker: Bookstore owner
blocks.content: It's over there, in the corner.
The bookstore owner, despite her face being hidden by large glasses and a scarf, recognized her immediately.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: There it is. The sequel to The Primal Snare.
A dark love story between a nobleman and a maid, devoid of any romance.
It was classified as adult material due to its decadent and explicit descriptions, but for Seriel, a lifelong reader of romance novels, it was nothing shocking.
From the very first page, the characters from the previous book were engaged in an intimate encounter.
Swallowing hard, she read on, completely engrossed in the story.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: The roles have reversed.
The maid, now holding a whip, approached the nobleman bound in chains.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Her breath made every hair on the man's body stand on end. Her tongue, moist like jelly slipping between her red lips, licked his nape…
Startled by the sudden voice, Seriel slammed the book shut.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Ah! Wh-who is it?
Turning, she saw a familiar face she didn't want to see.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Fermi?
As she lowered her large glasses to confirm, Fermi waved cheerfully.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Hello.
That same mischievous smile on his face.
